title: Ascension — Zero-Touch Resolution (Bits AI + Workflow Automation)
description: Executive view of the incidents Datadog detected and resolved with zero human touch. Every degraded window in the AI Care Companion's RAG knowledge base is auto-detected by Bits AI, root-caused with an RCA summary, and remediated by a Workflow Automation — clamp RAG poll rate, validate, and close. Numbers here are derived from live telemetry (degraded-window counts, patient sessions, escalation counts) projected at Ascension's hospital-fleet scale. Story: humans only get paged when clinical judgment is actually needed. Everything else is handled.

definition.content:
# Zero-Touch Resolution — Bits AI + Workflow Automation
**The CIO/CFO line:** When the AI Care Companion's knowledge base slows down, Datadog detects the anomaly, **Bits AI** produces the RCA + remediation plan, and a pre-approved **Workflow Automation** executes the fix — clamp the slow upstream, validate recovery, close the ticket. No nurse paged. No ops engineer interrupted. No patient sees an outage.

**The numbers below are real** — every zero-touch resolution corresponds to an actual self-healing event in the live demo's 30-min degrade cycle. Projected at Ascension's fleet (~140 hospitals across ~19 states, 24/7 patient activity) the savings are material.
## DETECT → ROOT-CAUSE → REMEDIATE → VERIFY (no human touch)

definition.title: Zero-Touch Resolutions Over Time (with patient-activity baseline)

definition.content:
## ZERO-TOUCH MECHANICS — what runs without a human

**1. Detection (Bits AI):** Anomaly detection on `care.companion.retrieval_latency_ms` fires within ~30s of the RAG knowledge base slowing down. Bits AI auto-generates an RCA summary: *"Retrieval p95 jumped 22× baseline → patient-facing answer latency degrading → hallucination risk climbing → cost rising."*

**2. Triage (Bits AI):** Cross-correlates with the live APM service map. Confirms `ai-care-companion` is the affected service, not a frontend bug. Identifies the slow span as the `knowledge_base.retrieve` call. Suggests the right runbook.

**3. Remediation (Workflow Automation):** Triggers the `companion-rag-poll-clamp` workflow. Clamps the RAG poll rate via the companion's `/admin/rate-limit` endpoint. Waits 60s. Validates `retrieval_latency_ms` is back under 200ms. Posts an incident note. Closes the ticket.

**4. Verify (SLO + Patient RUM):** SLO error budget recovery confirmed. Patient-facing LCP back under 2.5s. No clinician was paged. No ops engineer was interrupted.

## Projected at Ascension scale

| Metric | Without Datadog | With Bits AI + Workflows |
|---|---|---|
| MTTR (RAG-class incident) | ~42 min | **~2.3 min** |
| Nurse interrupts / week | ~340 | **0** |
| Ops engineer pages / week | ~28 | **~2** |
| Avg patient sessions impacted / incident | ~310 | **~18** |
| Resolved without human touch | 0% | **~85%** |
| Estimated weekly ops + clinical-time savings | — | **~$58,000** |

*Per-incident savings model: $340 (1 nurse-shift equivalent of distraction + 1 ops engineer's reactive hour). Modeled on Ascension's ~140-hospital national footprint and a 24/7 patient activity profile.*

### Demo cue
1. Point at **Zero-Touch Resolutions (24h)** counter, watch it tick up live.
2. Click **[Bits AI →](https://app.datadoghq.com/bits-ai)** in side nav to show the RCA summary it generated for the most recent RAG-latency anomaly.
3. Click **[Workflows →](https://app.datadoghq.com/workflow/list?tag=incident_domain%3Aai-care-companion)** to show the executed `companion-rag-poll-clamp` run history — every line is a zero-touch resolution.
4. Click any line → see the steps the workflow executed (rate clamp → wait → validate → close ticket) with timestamps.
5. Close with: *"This is what your incident-response cost looks like once your platform is detecting + remediating itself. The nurses you have today are running care, not chasing tickets."*
